Tests that multiplying `undefined` by `undefined` results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks if the result of multiplying `undefined` by `undefined` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The `assert` function is used to verify that the expression `isNaN(undefined * undefined) === true` evaluates to `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles operations involving `undefined` values, which should result in `NaN`. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific behavior of the JavaScript language.
